title: Transformation of competent E.coli cells with plasmid DNA
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"dta18","text":"For incubation on ice, make sure the tubes are standing in an ice-water mix, because without water, the cooling effect of ice is not reproducible due to the air between the ice fragments, especially if you have to incubate for a certain period of time.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"elele","text":"If you notice a significant drop in colony numbers after several transformations with plasmid DNA it's time to prepare fresh competent cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Thaw the appropriate amount of competent cells on ice.
Pre-chill the required number of empty 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tubes.
Pipet 50 µl aliquots of cells into the pre-chilled tubes.
Add 5-10 µl of a ligation reaction mix or 5 ng of pure plasmid DNA to each tube. Mix gently!
Incubate the tubes of ice for 30 min 00:30:00
Heat shock the cells for 45 sec at 42°C 00:00:45
Place the tubes immediately on ice for at least 2 min 00:02:00
Add 800 µl of SOC medium to each tube and incubate for 1 hour at 37°C 01:00:00
Transfer the cultures to 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tubes and spin for 1 min at 6000 rpm. 00:01:00
Remove 800 µl of the supernatant and resuspend the pellet.
Plate out the suspension on a LB agar plate containing the appropriate antibiotic.
creator: Lenny Teytelman
